perturbation.original_text: During each year of this agreement, Dr. Murray will be paid no later than the 30st day of January based on the cumulative Net Sales of the Dr. Murray Products for the preceding 12 months based upon the following scale: Net Sales per 12 Months Royalty $2,000,001 to $3,500,000 $150,000 $3,500,001 to $5,000,000 $250,000 $5,000,001 to $10,000,000 $350,000 $10,000,001 to $25,000,000 $500,000 $25,000,001 to $50,000,000 $1,000,000 $50,000,001 to $75,000,000 $1,500,000 $75,000,001 to $100,000,000 $2,000,000 Greater than $100,000,000 $2,500,000
perturbation.changed_text: During each year of this agreement, Dr. Murray will be paid no later than the 30th day of June based on the cumulative Net Sales of the Dr. Murray Products for the preceding 12 months based upon the following scale: Net Sales per 12 Months Royalty $2,000,001 to $3,500,000 $150,000 $3,500,001 to $5,000,000 $250,000 $5,000,001 to $10,000,000 $350,000 $10,000,001 to $25,000,000 $500,000 $25,000,001 to $50,000,000 $1,000,000 $50,000,001 to $75,000,000 $1,500,000 $75,000,001 to $100,000,000 $2,000,000 Greater than $100,000,000 $2,500,000
perturbation.explanation: Changing the payment deadline to June 30 may create a delay and therefore a violation in reporting requirements regarding income and sales.
perturbation.law_explanation: This code section and related IRS regulations require businesses to report payments of $600 or more annually to non-employees. Reporting usually involves Form 1099-NEC, which must be furnished to the recipient by January 31st of the following year. Changing the payment deadline could cause a delay in receiving information and non-compliance with information reporting requirements under federal tax law.

Information at source (a) Payments of $600 or more All persons engaged in a trade or business and making payment in the course of such trade or business to another person, of rent, salaries, wages, premiums, annuities, compensations, remunerations, emoluments,
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the payment deadline from January 31st to June 30th. The Law Explanation and Perturbation Explanation outline that this change could cause non-compliance with federal tax law, specifically reporting requirements that mandate information be furnished to the recipient by January 31st of the following year for payments of $600 or more (as indicated in Scraped Law Snippet 1, referencing 26 USC 6041). Therefore, the change directly contradicts the implied deadline for reporting requirements, constituting a violation.

perturbation.original_text: The mediation shall take place no more than sixty (60) days after written notice from the party requesting such mediation.
perturbation.changed_text: The mediation shall take place no more than fifteen (15) days after written notice from the party requesting such mediation.
perturbation.explanation: Reducing the mediation period to 15 days is not long enough to prepare for mediation and therefore contradicts the required notice needed for such a process.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Arizona Rules of Civil Procedure, Rule 16
perturbation.law_explanation: The Arizona Rules of Civil Procedure, Rule 16, addresses pretrial management and scheduling, including mediation. While it doesn't explicitly state a minimum period, a 15 day mediation preparation period will likely be insufficient and undermine the process, potentially violating the spirit and intent of fair and adequate preparation inherent in procedural rules. This would also be contradictory to the intent of mediation in Arizona which is meant to be an effective form of ADR.
perturbation.justification: The modified text reduces the mediation period from 60 to 15 days. The perturbation explanation states this reduction is insufficient for adequate preparation. The law explanation suggests this undermines the process and violates the spirit of fair preparation inherent in procedural rules. Although the scraped law snippets are missing, the definition of 'Inconsistencies Legal Contradiction' states that a contradiction exists when 'a contract sets a deadline or requirement that violates federal or state law'. The changed text creates an inconsistency that contradicts the intent of the law because reducing the mediation preparation time to 15 days would not give each party adequate time to prepare for such a mediation. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies Legal Contradiction' definition because the time-sensitive obligation in the contract does not align with legal requirements.

perturbation.original_text: The Services Term of this Agreement shall be automatically renewed for successive two-year terms thereafter unless written notice is given by either party to the other, indicating that party's intention not to renew the Services Term of this Agreement, at least ninety (90) days prior to the end of the initial Services Term or any renewed Services Term.
perturbation.changed_text: The Services Term of this Agreement shall be automatically renewed for successive two-year terms thereafter unless written notice is given by either party to the other, indicating that party's intention not to renew the Services Term of this Agreement, at least fifteen (15) days prior to the end of the initial Services Term or any renewed Services Term.
perturbation.explanation: The period required for giving notice of intention not to renew is too short.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Implied Covenant of Good Faith and Fair Dealing (Arizona Law)
perturbation.law_citation: Rawhide Western Town & Steakhouse, LLC v. Rawhide Western Town, Inc., 243 Ariz. 203, 403 P.3d 155 (Ct. App. 2017)
perturbation.law_explanation: Under Arizona law, every contract contains an implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing. This covenant requires parties to act honestly and not frustrate the purpose of the contract. Reducing the notice period to 15 days could be deemed a violation of this covenant if it makes it unreasonably difficult for the other party to prepare for the end of the services term or find a suitable replacement, thus frustrating the contract's purpose.
perturbation.justification: The original contract requires a 90-day notice for non-renewal, while the changed text reduces it to 15 days. The perturbation explanation states that this shorter period is problematic. The law explanation suggests this could violate the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing under Arizona law if the reduced notice period makes it unreasonably difficult for the other party to prepare for the end of the services term. Since the 'Inconsistencies Legal Contradiction' definition states that a contradiction exists when 'a contract sets a deadline or requirement that violates federal or state law', and reducing the notice period to 15 days could be interpreted as a violation of good faith, the change introduces a legal contradiction because the 15-day requirement could be considered unreasonably short under state law.
